description_long: This is a traditional wooden windmill with a hexagonal log base and a rectangular upper housing, topped by a pitched roof. The windmill features four large, cloth-covered blades attached to a central hub, which is mounted on the front of the structure. The wood appears weathered and dark, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The base is constructed from interlocking logs, providing a sturdy foundation, while the upper section is made from flat wooden planks. The overall design is rustic and utilitarian, typical of historical windmills found in rural or park settings. The windmill is likely non-operational and serves as a decorative or historical landmark in an urban park or open space.
description: A rustic wooden windmill with a hexagonal log base, pitched roof, and four large cloth-covered blades. The structure is weathered and traditional, evoking historical rural architecture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the pitched roof, hexagonal log base, and the thin profile of the windmill blades.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting all four large blades, the central hub, and the full face of the windmill's upper housing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the blades seen edge-on and the log base clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the pitched roof and the log base, with the windmill blades extending outward from the front.
